attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CVD (Cardiovascular) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CVD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as ~58 (adult band) — NO age is stated anywhere in the source note; clinician to confirm.

Sex inferred as female — NO sex is stated in the source note; post-pelvic-surgery unilateral lower-limb lymphoedema is most commonly post gynaecological-oncology surgery, hence the tentative inference. Clinician to confirm; if male (e.g. post-prostatectomy/pelvic node dissection), only demographics change, not the clinical content.
Provisional case_id SPEC-VASC-01-00225 — SEQ derived from source CDV-025; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-VASC-01 bucket.
Specialty assigned VASC (vascular/lymphatic) with secondary DERM — source corpus is CVD/Cardiovascular; reclassify if the maintainer prefers a different primary specialty.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source note's in-person examination (non-pitting oedema toes-to-groin, positive Stemmer's sign, measured 10x8 cm demarcated plaque, hyperkeratosis/peau d'orange, Doppler-palpable pulses) and clinician-measured vitals (BP 130/80, HR 88, RR 16, SpO2 98%) were sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; only patient-obtainable data (video-observable swelling/red patch, self-reported low-grade fever, home-thermometer 37.8 C, self-reported weight) were placed in 01.objective_data_offered, verified:false.

Baseline tier set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment), NOT T5 — patient is haemodynamically stable with only a low-grade fever and no systemic toxicity; the source clinician managed with ORAL antibiotics in the community. Escalation edges cover T4 (ED) for spread/access failure and T5 (000) for sepsis, PE features, or necrotising-infection signs.

Flucloxacillin 500 mg QID for 10 days is taken verbatim from the source note ('e.g., Flucloxacillin 500mg QID for 10 days'). Doses for the alternatives (cephalexin, clindamycin) were NOT invented — omitted pending the in-person prescribing encounter.

AMT/PBS item codes for flucloxacillin/cephalexin/clindamycin were NOT assigned (uncertain) — left for terminology-receipt resolution rather than emitting a plausible-but-wrong code; diagnosis SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes are registered as candidates (unverified).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
